content.full_text:
uFdil
ttlarc h 29, 1990
[,layne Imbery
State of California
Fair Pol itical Practices Cormission
P.0. Box 897
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Mr. Imbery:
I am writing to you on behalf of United Food and Cormercial Workers
Local 135, San Diego, Cal ifornia. I have recently been retained as the
attorney for the local and have been given the responsibility of
ensuring that all campaign disclosure requirements are met under the
Political Reform Act of 7974, as amended.
Although you and I have had many telephone conversations, I feel it
best to outline a history of the union's political activity so that you
may best advise us of our reporting obligations. It is ouLintention to
comply fully with the requirements of the Act and we seek your advice on
how best to do this.
r...:
a*..
United Food and Comnercial l,lorkers
which was formed in 1986 as a result of
Conmercial Uorkers Union Local L222 and
liorkers Union Local 229A.
Union Local 135 is a labor union
a merger between United Food and
United Food and Commerc i a I
At sometime prior to 1984, Local L222 f orned a Political Action
Comni ttee whose purpose was to contribute to state and local candidates.
The Committee filed a Statement of 0rganization, Form 410, as "UFCW,
Local 7222 Legislative Comnittee" hereinafter referred to as
ILegislative Committee", and received the identification nunber 830-021
from the State of California.
Contributions to the Legislative Committee were received through an
allocation or "earmarking" of membership dues in the amount of
twenty-five cents (250) per member per month. These were kept in a
separate, segregated fund apart from the Local's treasury funds.
0n June 14, 1984 the union formed the United Food and Commercial
Workers Union, Local 1222 Active Ballot Conmittee, hereinafter "ABC
Cornmittee", and filed a Statement of 0rganization with the Federal
Election Cormission (FEC), FEC #C00186338, This cormittee was formed
for the purpose of contributing to candidates in federal elections. The
United Food & Commercial \ orkers
Local 135, AFL-CIO & CLC
2oo1 Caminodel Bio Soulh
San Di€go, Calllornia92108
1619) 298.77/2
Thomas J. Vandeveld


ABC Committee receives voluntary contributions from one hundred or more
persons quarter'ly.
During the period from June 14, 1984 through June 30, 1984, the
Committee received $1706.00 in voluntary "Active Ballot contributions,,
(not dues). These were placed in a separate, segregated fund apart from
general treasury funds, and the Legislative Committee funds. These
"Active Ballot contributions" were reported to the FEC. The ABC
Cornmi ttee continued to receive voluntary "Active Ballot contribut.ions"
(not dues) and make disbursements in thi: form of operating expenditures.
These were al1 duly reported to the FEC.
0n March 26, 1985, the Legislative Committee was terminated and all
surplus funds reverted to the Local's treasury. A Statement of
Termination, Form 415, was filed with the State of California.
0n September 16, 1986, the ABC Committee contributed to four
congressional candidate committees. The ABC Committee also contributed
to the UFC[,J International Active Ballot C1ub, located in Washington,
D. C.
0n September 25, 1986, the ABC Committee contributed to five
congressional candidate corrnittees. All contributions were duly
reported to the FEC.
0n September 25, 1986, the ABC Comnittee contributed to two state
committees. These contributions were reported to the FEC, with a copy
sent to the Secretary of the State of California.
During the period of July 1, 1987 through June 30, 1988, the ABC
Committee made two contributions to a single congressional candidate and
made an independent expenditure for a single newspaper advertisement
supporting a senatorial and a congressional candidate. These were duly
reported to the FEC.
0n July 1, 1988, the ABC Commi ttee made a contribution to a
California State Assembly candidate. This contribution was reported to
the FEC with a copy to the Secretary of the State of Californ.ia. 0n
0ctober 3, 1988, the committee contributed to ten congressional
candidates. 0n 0ctober 31, 1988, the Committee made an independent
expenditure on behalf of several candidates in federa.l elections, 0n
November 3, 1988, the Conrni ttee contributed to the UFCW International
Committee and on November 4, 1988, the ABC Conrmi ttee was refunded one of
the 0ctober 3, 1988 contributions. All of these expenditures were duly
reported to the FEC,
During the period from January 1' 1989 through June 30, 1989, the
ABC Commi ttee made a contribution to a State Assembly candidate by
buying tickets to a fundraiser. This was reported as "other
diibuisements" on the appropriate FEC form. The Committee also made a
contribution to a congressional candjdate and voided a check to another
congressional candidaie. The check had been lost. The Committee also
mad6 an independent expenditure on behalf of several federal candidates
in the form of a newspaper advertisement.


During the period from July 1, 1989 through December 31, 1989, the
union (not the Conrnittee) made independent expenditures on behalf of
1oca1 city council candidates. These expenditures were made out of
general treasury funds that were not "earmarked" for politica-l
contribution or expenditures. This is the fund into which dues money is
deposited and is separate from the committee fund. These expenditures
were reported on fonn 465 (Supplemental Independent Expenditures
Report ) .
In addition, the ABC Conmittee made a contribution to a state
senatorial candidate on August 25, 1989. This contribution was reported
to the FEC with a copy to the Secretary of the State of California.
As of the present date, it appears that all political expenditures
wili henceforth be made out of voluntary contributions received by the
ABC Conmittee. The union does not anticipate making further political
expenditures out of its general fund.
As the Active Ballot Committee would like to contribute to state
and local candidates out of Active Ballot Committee funds, we need to
know our reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act of 7974,
as amended. Could you please answer the following questions?
1. Are we required to file a Statement of Organization with the
State of Cal ifornia for the committee?
2. If so, what would be the date the committee was fomed?
3. 
Does the comnittee qualify as a "broad-based political
committee"? If so, when did it qualify?
4. Is the corflmi ttee required to file campaign statements for past
contributions to state candidates and/or conrnittees?
5. If the committee decides to form a slate-mailer organization,
what reporting obligations must be met?
6. If the slate-mailer or mailers are paid for in part by
paynents from candidates or their cormittees, and paid for in part by
the slate-mailer organization, is there an allocation of monies spent
between persons or ballot measures appearing on the slate-mailer? How
would this be required to be reported?
Please respond in writing. Thank you for your cooperation and
assistance.
Sincerely,
Tom Vandevel d, III
Legal Counsel
UFCW Local 135
,
TV:ls

sections.question:
1. Are we required to file a Statement of Organization with the State of California for the committee?
2. If so, what would be the date the committee was formed?
3. Does the committee qualify as a "broad-based political committee"? If so, when did it qualify?
4. Is the committee required to file campaign statements for past contributions to state candidates and/or committees?
5. If the committee decides to form a slate-mailer organization, what reporting obligations must be met?
6. If the slate-mailer or mailers are paid for in part by payments from candidates or their committees, and paid for in part by the slate-mailer organization, is there an allocation of monies spent between persons or ballot measures appearing on the slate-mailer? How would this be required to be reported?
sections.question_synthetic: An attorney representing UFCW Local 135 seeks clarification on the union's campaign disclosure reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act of 1974, including questions about Statement of Organization filing requirements, broad-based political committee status, past contribution reporting, and slate-mailer organization reporting obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document contains a detailed narrative history of the union's political committees and activities (Legislative Committee, ABC Committee) from 1984-1989, followed by six numbered questions for FPPC guidance. No conclusion or FPPC response is present in this document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Are we required to file a Statement of Organization with the State of California for the committee?
2. If so, what would be the date the committee was formed?
3. Does the committee qualify as a "broad-based political committee"? If so, when did it qualify?
4. Is the committee required to file campaign statements for past contributions to state candidates and/or committees?
5. If the committee decides to form a slate-mailer organization, what reporting obligations must be met?
6. If the slate-mailer or mailers are paid for in part by payments from candidates or their committees, and paid for in part by the slate-mailer organization, is there an allocation of monies spent between persons or ballot measures appearing on the slate-mailer? How would this be required to be reported?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from legal counsel for UFCW Local 135 to the FPPC outlining the union's political activity history and seeking written advice on six specific questions regarding campaign disclosure requirements under California's Political Reform Act, including organization filing, committee qualification, and slate-mailer reporting obligations.
